text: Bias-variance tradeoff: high-bias models (e.g., overly simple linear models) underfit, while high-variance models (e.g., overly complex models) overfit. Regularization, more data, and cross-validation help manage this tradeoff.

Overfitting occurs when a model fits noise in the training data and performs poorly on unseen data. Indicators include a large gap between training and validation performance. Mitigations include regularization, early stopping, data augmentation, and simpler models.

In Transformers, self-attention computes a weighted sum of value vectors, where weights are derived from query-key similarity. Multi-head attention allows the model to attend to information from different representation subspaces.

Vector search retrieves documents by embedding queries and documents into the same vector space and performing similarity search (e.g., cosine similarity). FAISS is a library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors.
FAISS IndexFlatIP performs inner-product search. If embeddings are L2-normalized, inner product corresponds to cosine similarity, which is commonly used for semantic retrieval.
